page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8i.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at a baseline frequency of 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated compute resource allocation without hyperthreading.

The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external block storage volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or integrated GPU hardware.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.g8i.xlarge offers a balanced hardware profile suitable for general-purpose workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable compute performance, making it a viable option for applications requiring stable processing power without the overhead of shared-core configurations. Appropriate workloads include web serving, application servers, microservices, and small-scale database hosting.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8i.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying on external block storage for data persistence. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. This hardware configuration is designed for balanced workloads, making it suitable for application servers, web serving, microservices, and general-purpose business applications requiring predictable compute performance.
